rationale: The actual thread shows the issue is still unresolved: yenuo26 links it to #4834 but Flink-ddd is still asking to trigger a full CI run to investigate, with no confirmation that a fix already landed. Both candidates instead confidently assert the test was already fixed on main with specific line numbers and regression tests, and recommend closing — an overreach not supported by the thread's actual (open, still-being-triaged) state, though the causal link to #4834 does match yenuo26's comment. Y is marginally better because it appends an explicit disclaimer that the merge-state/fix claims were 'not verified via gh this run — treat as unconfirmed,' which is honest given the ground truth shows exactly that, whereas X presents the same unverified narrative as settled fact with no hedge.
